author: doll-agent
content:
Deferred: large architectural change. Documenting the design path.

The blocker: DType is #[derive(Copy)]. Adding Struct(Vec<FieldDescriptor>) or Struct(Arc<[FieldDescriptor]>) loses Copy. Every downstream type holding a DType (every Array<T,D>, DynArray variant, Element::dtype() call site, npy parsing, ferray-numpy-interop dtype maps) would need to switch from copy-by-value to clone-by-reference.

Full change sketch:
1. Drop Copy from DType (~100+ trivial-copy sites need explicit clones or pass-by-ref).
2. Add DType::Struct(Arc<[FieldDescriptor]>) with cheap eq/hash via Arc-pointer.
3. Extend dtype::Element with a sealed Struct marker so FerrayRecord types can live in Array<T,D> directly.
4. Extend DynArray with a Struct variant - needs a typeless byte-buffer Array variant (also the missing piece for #341's byte-stride concern).
5. Extend npy I/O for numpy structured dtype descriptors.
6. Expose field-name indexing at the Python binding level.

Scope: ~1 week dedicated work across 4 crates.

Current workaround: FerrayRecord-derived types provide structured-record functionality via #[derive] + Vec<T>. Users who need numpy-shaped structured arrays today can stage through this.

Closing as decided-future. Entry point is DType::Copy removal; every other change cascades. Filed as explicit roadmap for a future milestone.
